def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    df['bb_upper'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_hband(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    df['bb_lower'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_lband(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    df['bb_width'] = (df['bb_upper'] - df['bb_lower']) / df['close'].rolling(params['bb_window']).mean()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window'])
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype='int')
    
    # Long condition: BB width below threshold and RSI oversold
    long_cond = (df['bb_width'] < params['bb_width_threshold']) & (df['rsi'] < params['rsi_lower'])
    signals[long_cond] = 1
    
    # Short condition: BB width below threshold and RSI overbought
    short_cond = (df['bb_width'] < params['bb_width_threshold']) & (df['rsi'] > params['rsi_upper'])
    signals[short_cond] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: USD/JPY exhibits explosive moves after periods of low volatility, with directional bias confirmed by RSI extremes during compression phases.
